key_points: Source/Action: Adopted by City Council of San José (Resolution, Agenda Item 8.2(c)(2), September 1, 2020).
Affected Development: Non-Residential Projects and non-residential portions of mixed projects (Office, Retail, Hotel, Industrial/Research and Development, Warehouse, Residential Care).
Fee Rate - Office (<100,000 sq. ft.): $3.00 per sq. ft.
Fee Rate - Retail (≥100,000 sq. ft.): $3.00 per sq. ft.
Fee Rate - Retail (<100,000 sq. ft.): No fee ($0).
Fee Rate - Hotel: $5.00 per sq. ft.
Fee Rate - Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq. ft.): $3.00 per sq. ft.
Fee Rate - Industrial/Research and Development (<100,000 sq. ft.): No fee ($0).
Fee Rate - Warehouse: $5.00 per sq. ft.
Fee Rate - Residential Care: $18.706.00 per sq. ft. (Text anomaly present, likely extraction error in source).
Credits: Demolition of legally permitted non-residential square footage grants a credit per sq. ft. based on the fee of the prior use.
Payment Timing: Not specified in the provided evidence.
Pass-Through Incidence: Evidence contains no assumptions or data regarding indirect pass-through to households.

top_results.snippet: The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing. The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use. The San José City Council voted on December 11, 2018 to pursue a nexus and a feasibility study on a potential commercial linkage fee in San José. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development(≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply. | Industrial/Research and Development (≥100,000 sq.ft.) | $3.58 - When paid in full prior to the Building Permit issuance, a 20% reduction in the payment will apply.
top_results.title: Commercial Linkage Fee | City of San José

top_results.snippet: Following the nexus study, local city councils set fees at levels expected to generate resources for local affordable housing development without making
top_results.title: Commercial Linkage Fees

top_results.snippet: As cities build jobs-generating commercial real estate – like new office buildings, retail space, or warehouses – a CLF requires commercial developers to pay a fee to offset a small portion of the need for affordable housing created by new jobs. Before levying an impact fee, jurisdictions are required by state law to complete a nexus study that shows the linkage between the new development and the increased demand for the facilities. Following the nexus study, local city councils set fees at levels expected to generate resources for local affordable housing development without making commercial development infeasible. On Tuesday, September 1st, 2020, the San Jose City Council approved a Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Ordinance, joining all of the other major cities in the South Bay that have already adopted fees. City of San Jose Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance – September 1, 2020. * City of Mountain View 2013 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance. * City of Sunnyvale 2015 Commercial Linkage Fee Ordinance.
